chapters.chapter_summary: The video begins with an introduction and transitions to a graphic overview of the anatomy, suggesting the patient was positioned appropriately for the procedure.
Three incision points were marked near the navel and lower right side of the abdomen for laparoscopic surgery instruments.
Surgical tools were inserted at the marked points, and the abdomen was inflated with gas to facilitate exposure of the internal organs.
Robotic arms manipulated tissues around the cystic duct, leading to the removal of the gallbladder.
Surgical suction was used to remove excess fluid post-gallbladder extraction, ensuring hemostasis.
The incisions were closed with sutures, followed by skin glue or skin closure tape.
The video concluded with a message about potential complications and the need for open surgery if necessary.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a laparoscopic cholecystectomy on a patient with gallstones and gallbladder disease. The procedure was conducted under general anesthesia with the patient in a supine position.
SOAP.Objective: I identified the liver and gallbladder anatomy, observed gallstones within the gallbladder, and successfully dissected the gallbladder from surrounding tissues. I inflated the abdomen with gas to facilitate exposure and used robotic arms to manipulate tissues around the cystic duct.
SOAP.Assessment: The procedure was completed without visible complications. Hemostasis was achieved, and the gallbladder was placed in a specimen bag. The patient's recovery appeared stable, with no signs of intraoperative bleeding or injury to adjacent structures.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the incisions with sutures and applied skin glue or skin closure tape. I prepared the patient for postoperative care, monitoring for any signs of complications such as infection or bile leakage.
